summary:
• Upcoming Election Registration Event: Scheduled for Saturday, 2:00 PM - 4:00 PM, aimed at facilitating voter registration for the August 6 local elections.
  • Teen Artist Showcase: Running from June 26 - July 30, this free exhibit promotes youth creativity and community engagement, culminating in a reception on July 30.
  • Wastewater Infrastructure Update: Progress on the e2i2 Equalization Station indicates that new wastewater storage tanks are nearing completion, enhancing city infrastructure.

items.title: Our Election Registration event is almost here!🗳️ This Saturday, from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM, we will be helping...
items.content_text:
Our Election Registration event is almost here!🗳️
 This Saturday, from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM, we will be helping community members register for the upcoming local elections on August 6. There will be FREE ice cream, kids' activities, community resources and much more, so don't miss it!💚 You can also stop by Café del Sur in the morning to receive useful information about voter registration and sweet bread.☕️
 This event is organized by United by Change Coalition - Hamilton County, TN, CALEB - Chattanooga in Action for Love, Equality and Benevolence and La Paz Chattanooga.

items.title: Today, we're continuing our Exploring Downtown series with Main Terrain Art Park. Tucked into Chattanooga’s Southside, M...
items.content_text:
Today, we're continuing our Exploring Downtown series with Main Terrain Art Park.
 Tucked into Chattanooga’s Southside, Main Terrain Art Park is a creative example of how art, recreation, and sustainability can come together to build an active public space. What was once a vacant, underutilized lot at the end of Main Street was transformed into a 1.72-acre public art and fitness park through a partnership between the City of Chattanooga, local organizations, and support from the National Endowment for the Arts. The park’s signature sculptural elements, designed by artist Thomas Sayre, reflect the architecture of the Walnut Street Bridge. 
 As you explore the park, you’ll find interactive art, fitness features, walking paths, and stormwater management systems woven into the landscape. Next time you're in Downtown Chattanooga, take a walk through this unique landmark!

items.title: There's still time to sign up for our Tai Chi classes at the Chris Ramsey Sr. Center and the East Lake Center! Check the...
items.content_text:
There's still time to sign up for our Tai Chi classes at the Chris Ramsey Sr. Center and the East Lake Center! Check the comments for sign up!
Looking for a way to improve your strength, balance, and overall well-being? Join us for our FREE Tai Chi for Fall Prevention and Arthritis classes at the East Lake Community Center in July and the Chris Ramsey Sr. Community Center in September! 
 Led by certified instructors, these gentle, evidence-based classes can help reduce arthritis symptoms, improve balance, and lower the risk of falls. The program is designed for seniors, but adults of all fitness levels can benefit from this relaxing form of exercise. Space is limited, and registration is required. CC for details
 https://forms.office.com/pages/responsepage.aspx…

items.title: How big are the new wastewater storage tanks? Come with us as we take an exclusive look inside…  Checking back in on the...
items.content_text:
How big are the new wastewater storage tanks? Come with us as we take an exclusive look inside… 
 Checking back in on the e2i2 Equalization Station off of I-75, the equalization tanks are almost ready for use. The West Chickamauga EQ Site features two 15 million gallon tanks for storing excess flow in the sanitary sewer system during heavy rainfall events. This additional storage capacity will prevent sanitary sewer overflows (SSOs) into South Chickamauga Creek and the Tennessee River. 
 Thank you to the Chattanooga Wastewater Department for the hard work toward this public safety initiative!

items.title: Early detection saves lives. The Hamilton County Health Department provides Women’s Health Services to help you prioriti...
items.content_text:
Early detection saves lives.
 The Hamilton County Health Department provides Women’s Health Services to help you prioritize your health. Taking care of yourself is essential not only for you but also for those who care about you.
 Our services include, but are not limited to, well-woman visits, vaccinations, and age-appropriate exams.
 All of our locations offer Women’s Health Services. To find the location that works best for you, please visit http://health.hamiltontn.org/…/sexualreproductivehealthclin… or call (423) 209-8009.

items.title: Strike one. Strike two. Strike three... BOOTED. Starting October 1st, vehicles with 3+ outstanding parking tickets 60 da...
items.content_text:
Strike one. Strike two. Strike three... BOOTED.
 Starting October 1st, vehicles with 3+ outstanding parking tickets 60 days or older are eligible for booting.
 Good news: you've still got time to step up to the plate. Pay your outstanding tickets by June 30th and get 35% OFF your total balance.
 Don't let this be a swing and a miss.
 Visit www.chattanoogaparking.org to pay your outstanding tickets.

items.title: What's better than butter & baguettes? 🧈 This Friday night, join Scenic City Clay Arts and The Chattery for a creative...
items.content_text:
What's better than butter & baguettes? 🧈
 This Friday night, join Scenic City Clay Arts and The Chattery for a creative pottery class featuring a fresh baguette from Oui Oui Boulangerie. 
 In this class, you'll handmake a ceramic butter dish designed to elevate everyday meals. SCCA's instructor Summer Tomes will guide you through each step, from shaping and carving to adding unique surface textures and decorative details. 
 While you won't get to take home your butter dish immediately after class (it'll be ready in 6 - 8 weeks), you will get to take home a delicious French baguette. 🥖
 ❗️Ticket sales end on 6/24 (and they're going fast).❗️Sign up here: https://thechattery.org/…/butter-baguettes-make-your-own-ce….

items.title: The Chattanooga Public Library is just 1 week away!📚 ​​Visit our offices on Tuesday, June 30...
items.content_text:
We're only 1 week away from visiting The Chattanooga Public Library!📚
 Visit our offices on Tuesday, June 30, from 10 AM to 12 PM, to register for a library card and learn about all the free services they offer!🥳
 @followers
Learn about the Chattanooga Public Library at this FREE event!📚
 Poppy, The Chattanooga Public Library's bookmobile, will be giving us a fun tour on Tuesday, June 30, from 10 AM to 12 PM. 🌞 
 At this event you can learn how to register to obtain a public library card, and obtain the possibility of renting books, utensils, participating in activities and crafts, much more and all at NO COST.🤩
 The event will be at the La Paz offices, located at 809 S Willow St, Chattanooga, TN. There is no registration required, everyone is welcome to come with their families!

items.title: Congratulations to Taziki's Mediterranean Cafe on their recent ribbon cutting! 🎀✂️ Taziki's Mediterranean Cafe on Market Street is...
items.content_text:
Congratulations to Taziki's Mediterranean Cafe on their recent ribbon cutting! 🎀✂️ 

Taziki's Mediterranean Cafe on Market Street is a locally owned restaurant serving fresh, Mediterranean-inspired meals in the heart of downtown Chattanooga. Known for its grilled meats, fresh salads, gyros, sandwiches, and signature dips, Taziki's has been a part of the downtown community since 2011 and continues to be a favorite spot for lunch, dinner, catering, and gathering with friends and family.  

📍 432 Market St.
🔗 https://lnkd.in/eTtvH-nr

 The City of Red Bank is now accepting applications for the 2026 ARPA Fund Non-Profit Organization Grants. This funding opportunity supports programs, projects, and services that make a positive impact in our community.

 Additional information may be requested from Leslie Slay at lslay@redbanktn.gov

items.title: 🚧 Dog Park Sidewalk Closure Notice 🐾 Heads up, everyone! The Dog Park sidewalk will be closed starting today through F...
items.content_text:
🚧 Dog Park Sidewalk Closure Notice 🐾
 Heads up, everyone! The Dog Park sidewalk will be closed starting today through Friday, June 26 for a full replacement. Crews will be working throughout the week to improve safety and accessibility.
 The dog park itself remains open, but please use extra caution and follow any posted detours when visiting.
 Thanks for your patience while we make these upgrades for our community and our four‑legged friends!

items.title: LET'S KICK IT! The 2nd annual PAL Kickball Turf Wars is just one month away, and sign ups are open now! We're looking fo...
items.content_text:
LET'S KICK IT!
 The 2nd annual PAL Kickball Turf Wars is just one month away, and sign ups are open now! We're looking for kids between the ages of 12 and 18 to go head to head (or should we say foot to foot?) with our cops in this tournament. Participation is completely free, and all skill levels are welcome. For more information, contact Sgt. Robin Davenport at (423) 544-2841 or rdavenport@chattanooga.gov
 Sign up here: https://hubs.la/Q04mdp_90
 Event Details
 🔹July 22, 2026
 🔹 1:00 PM
 🔹Finley Stadium (1826 Reggie White Blvd)

items.title: 🦋 Help the Monarch Butterfly Thrive in Tennessee! 🦋 The City of Red Bank Community Development Department is proud to...
items.content_text:
🦋 Help the Monarch Butterfly Thrive in Tennessee! 🦋
 The City of Red Bank Community Development Department is proud to support the Tennessee Department of Transportation's (TDOT) Project Milkweed initiative, a statewide effort to restore habitat for the iconic monarch butterfly and other important pollinators.
 https://tnpollinators.org/milkweed/
 Monarch butterflies depend on milkweed plants for survival. In fact, milkweed is the only plant where monarchs lay their eggs and the only food source for monarch caterpillars. As monarch populations continue to decline across North America, planting native milkweed and pollinator-friendly flowers can make a meaningful difference.
 Whether in your backyard, school garden, business landscape, or community space, adding native plants helps create the habitat monarch butterflies need during their incredible migration journey through Tennessee.
 Supporting pollinators also supports a healthier environment, greater biodiversity, and a more beautiful community for future generations.
 Together, we can help ensure that the monarch butterfly remains a treasured part of Tennessee's natural heritage.
 🌿 Learn more about Project Milkweed and consider planting native species in your landscape this season.
 #ProjectMilkweed #MonarchButterfly #PollinatorHabitat #NativePlants #RedBankTN #CommunityDevelopment #EnvironmentalStewardship #KeepTennesseeBeautiful

items.title: Join the conversation on trust in public institutions.   Hear what people across 38 countries had to say as we launch th...
items.content_text:
Join the conversation on trust in public institutions.  
 Hear what people across 38 countries had to say as we launch the OECD Survey on Drivers of Trust in Public Institutions 2026 Results. 
 This report provides new evidence on how trust in public institutions has evolved since 2021. It also examines the implications for improving public governance and delivering better outcomes for citizens. 
 The event will feature opening remarks by OECD Secretary-General Mathias Cormann with a presentation of the results of the survey by Director for Public Governance at the OECD,  Elsa Pilichowski.
 This event will offer valuable insights for policymakers, practitioners and researchers working on public governance and institutional reform. 
 Please register to attend: https://brnw.ch/21x3zn3  
 #OECDTrustSurvey

items.title: A championship coach. A mentor. A Moc legend. 🐐 After 25 seasons leading Chattanooga Mocs Softball, Coach Frank Reed ha...
items.content_text:
A championship coach. A mentor. A Moc legend. 🐐 After 25 seasons leading Chattanooga Mocs Softball, Coach Frank Reed has announced his retirement.
 His record speaks for itself: 824 wins, 10 Southern Conference regular-season titles, 11 tournament championships, and 11 NCAA Tournament appearances. But his greatest impact can be found in the thousands of student-athletes, coaches, and families whose lives he helped shape along the way.
 Thank you, Coach Reed, for 25 remarkable years of service to UTC. Your legacy of excellence, leadership, and care for student-athletes will continue to inspire future generations of Mocs.
 Read more on GoMocs.com: https://gomocs.com/…/softball-reed-retires-with-a-champions…

items.title: 🔧 Storm Update: We're Working for You A powerful storm with high winds has caused widespread damage across the Chattano...
items.content_text:
🔧 Storm Update: We're Working for You
 A powerful storm with high winds has caused widespread damage across the Chattanooga area — including more than 260 downed power lines, broken poles, and other damage. Our crews mobilized immediately, and we're not stopping until every customer is back on.
 Here's where things stand:
 ⚡ 11,700 customers avoided a lasting outage thanks to Chattanooga's automated smart grid, which prevented or auto-restored their power.
 👷 EPB is in the process of deploying 400+ utility workers, including additional crews we've called in from neighboring communities to help speed things up.
 🏠 We expect to restore thousands of customers by morning, but with damage this severe full restoration will require continuous effort through tomorrow.
 To our customers still waiting: thank you for your patience. Our teams are working around the clock — through the night and into tomorrow — to get your lights back on as quickly and safely as possible. 💙
 Stay safe, steer clear of downed lines, and report outages using the free MyEPB app.
items.content_html:
🔧 Storm Update: We're Working for You
A powerful storm with high winds has caused widespread damage across the Chattanooga area — including more than 260 downed power lines, broken poles, and other damage. Our crews mobilized immediately, and we're not stopping until every customer is back on.
Here's where things stand:
⚡ 11,700 customers avoided a lasting outage thanks to Chattanooga's automated smart grid, which prevented or auto-restored their power.
👷 EPB is in the process of deploying 400+ utility workers, including additional crews we've called in from neighboring communities to help speed things up.
🏠 We expect to restore thousands of customers by morning, but with damage this severe full restoration will require continuous effort through tomorrow.
To our customers still waiting: thank you for your patience. Our teams are working around the clock — through the night and into tomorrow — to get your lights back on as quickly and safely as possible. 💙
Stay safe, steer clear of downed lines, and report outages using the free MyEPB app.

items.title: Please share to help spread the word!A #TNSilverAlert has been issued on behalf of the Chattanooga Police Department for...
items.content_text:
Please share to help spread the word!
A #TNSilverAlert has been issued on behalf of the Chattanooga Police Department for 83-year-old Betty Miller. 
 Betty was last seen on Monday leaving her home in Chattanooga and is believed to be traveling in a 2008 Red Chevy Cobalt with TN tag PD69088.
 Betty is 5’4” tall, 165 lbs., with gray hair and brown eyes. She has a medical condition that may impair her ability to return safely without assistance.
  If you have seen Betty please call the Chattanooga Police Department at 423-698-2525 or TBI at 1-800-TBI-FIND.

From patriotic celebrations and colorful tie-dye creations to movie nights under the stars and action-packed field day fun, something exciting is happening all month long. items.title: Chattanooga was blasted by storms Monday night (6/22/26), making things very busy for Red Shift firefighters across the...
items.content_text:
Chattanooga was blasted by storms Monday night (6/22/26), making things very busy for Red Shift firefighters across the city. Calls quickly poured into 911, like wires down, fires, flooding, and large trees on homes. We have no injuries to report at this time, thankfully. The structural damage caused by downed trees displaced several families. Trees fell in multiple locations, including: 
 4100 block of Kirkland Avenue
 300 block of S Howell Avenue
 800 block of Mount Vernon Circle 
 2000 block of N Chamberlain Avenue
 3300 block of Adkins Road 
 Please do not drive through flooded roadways or drive over downed power lines.

items.title: No, we’re not building an underground water park. 😎 Today we got a look inside the tanks being built along I-75 near ou...
items.content_text:
No, we’re not building an underground water park. 😎
 Today we got a look inside the tanks being built along I-75 near our Brown Acres Golf Course  These giant tanks along I-75 are actually part of a major project that will store up to 30 million gallons of stormwater during heavy rains. Think of them as Chattanooga’s version of a giant “pause button” for excess water and help clean. 
 They may look like something out of a sci-fi movie now, but by 2027 they’ll help protect our water system and keep local waterways cleaner like the South Chickamauga Creek.
 Now, this project will not be complete till fall of 2027, which means the greenway from Camp Jordan to Brained Levee will remain closed till then. 
 Also, if anyone asks, yes… we checked. Echoes are incredible in there. 🎤😂l

No, we’re not building an underground water park. 😎
Today we got a look inside the tanks being built along I-75 near our Brown Acres Golf Course These giant tanks along I-75 are actually part of a major project that will store up to 30 million gallons of stormwater during heavy rains. Think of them as Chattanooga’s version of a giant “pause button” for excess water and help clean.
They may look like something out of a sci-fi movie now, but by 2027 they’ll help protect our water system and keep local waterways cleaner like the South Chickamauga Creek.
Now, this project will not be complete till fall of 2027, which means the greenway from Camp Jordan to Brained Levee will remain closed till then.
Also, if anyone asks, yes… we checked. Echoes are incredible in there. 🎤😂l

items.title: Brainerd Hills, your new pavilion at Benham-Williams Park is officially open!  Huge kudos to everyone who worked to pull...
items.content_text:
Brainerd Hills, your new pavilion at Benham-Williams Park is officially open! 
 Huge kudos to everyone who worked to pull this off! This is the first "in-house" pavilion completely designed and constructed by our world-class team. Thank you to District 6 Councilwoman Jenni Berz and the Brainerd Hills Neighborhood Association for rallying the neighborhood for a rainy-day ribbon cutting and cookout in the park! It was a great chance to put this structure to the test, and it passed with flying colors. We are looking forward to opening more great structures like this in parks across our beautiful city.

items.title: In the modern world, where digital information is always at risk, security is more important than ever. UTC Research Ass...
items.content_text:
In the modern world, where digital information is always at risk, security is more important than ever.
 UTC Research Assistant Professor Eric Hoang H. Nguyen has always been interested in protecting information.
 “It’s a little bit of my habit,” Nguyen said. “I want to learn about a system, find problems with the system and try to solve the problems. That’s why I chose the topic I did, as it’s most relevant to security.
 🔗  Read more here: https://blog.utc.edu/…/behind-the-research-at-utc-dr-eric-…/

items.title: Earlier today, around 9:30 A.M., HCSO deputies responded to reports of a suspicious vehicle in the vicinity of Hixson Pi...
items.content_text:
Earlier today, around 9:30 A.M., HCSO deputies responded to reports of a suspicious vehicle in the vicinity of Hixson Pike and Thrasher Pike. After locating the vehicle, deputies made contact with the driver, who then fled, initiating a pursuit. 
 The pursuit ended at the Redoubt Soccer Complex, where deputies apprehended the suspect and took him into custody, transporting him to the Hamilton County Jail and Detention Center.
 Antonio Pickett was found to have active warrants and is now facing additional charges:
 Active Warrants:
 -  Reckless Endangerment
 -  Theft of Property
 -  Auto Burglary
 -  Evading Arrest
 New Charges:
 -  Evading Arrest
 -  Resisting Stop, Frisk, Halt, and Arrest
 -  Reckless Driving
 -  Driving on a Revoked Driver’s License 
 -  Vandalism 
 -  Possession of a Controlled Substance for Distribution (x2)
 -  Possession of Drug Paraphernalia 
 Additional traffic charges are pending.
 This information is preliminary and subject to change. No further details are available.

items.title: City of East Ridge Receives 2026 Community Service Award for Tourism Attraction! 🏆 Last week, the City of East Ridge wa...
items.content_text:
City of East Ridge Receives 2026 Community Service Award for Tourism Attraction! 🏆
 Last week, the City of East Ridge was honored to have been recognized by the Greater Chattanooga Hospitality Association- GCHA with the 2026 Community Service Award for Tourism Attraction in recognition of the East Ridge Parks and Recreation Department. 🙌🏼 
 While Camp Jordan serves as a major destination for sports tourism, outdoor recreation, festivals, and special events, this recognition reflects the broader impact of the East Ridge Parks & Recreation Department and the many ways it serves both residents and visitors throughout the entire community in #EastRidgeTN. 🤍
 From hosting regional tournaments and community events at Camp Jordan Park, to providing year-round programs and services through the East Ridge Community Center, and welcoming guests at the recently opened Venue 1921 at East Ridge, our Parks & Recreation Department continues to enhance quality of life while supporting tourism, economic activity, and community engagement here in our own community every single day! 
 This award would not be possible without the dedication of our Parks & Recreation staff, the support of Mayor Brian Williams and City Councilmembers, the collaboration of City departments, and the many community partners, volunteers, coaches, organizations, and residents who help make these facilities and programs successful in every way possible.
 As we continue investing in our parks, recreation facilities, community programming, and public spaces, we remain committed to creating opportunities that bring people together and showcase all that East Ridge has to offer! 
 Please join us in congratulating the East Ridge Parks & Recreation Department on this outstanding achievement! 👏🏼
 Let’s keep moving forward, East Ridge!
